executiveSummary.headline: The model reduced daily error versus TEPCO.
executiveSummary.summary: 24 comparable hours were available. Model MAE was 325.6 MW, TEPCO MAE was 557.1 MW. WAPE was 1.32% for the model and 2.27% for TEPCO.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: The model was closer than TEPCO on the daily view.
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: The model was more stable by daily MAE/WAPE. Track whether the same condition repeats.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: A large single-hour miss occurred.
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: The max-error hour had a large impact on daily metrics. Inspect lag, weather, and calibration metadata for that hour first.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: Peak timing was missed.
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: The model peak hour differed from the actual peak hour. Review weather deltas and lag shape around the peak.

rootCauseHypotheses.title: Actual peak level was underestimated.
rootCauseHypotheses.explanation: At the actual peak hour, the model forecast was low. Check cooling/heating load and same-business-type anchors.

featureRecommendations.suggestion: Review the impact of lgbm_quantile_q50 based on the hypothesis: The model was closer than TEPCO on the daily view.
featureRecommendations.expectedEffect: Check whether MAE, WAPE, and RMSE improve together under repeated hour-band and business-type conditions.
featureRecommendations.risk: Overfitting a single-day pattern can cause over-correction in other seasons or day types.
featureRecommendations.validationPlan: Compare WAPE, RMSE, and max error against TEPCO using recent 2-4 week replay and backtests.
